usage: trim_pg [-help] [-keep_dangling_via] -net {list_of_nets} { {-layer {<layer_name1> [<layer_name2>]} -pattern <trimming_pattern> -type {stripe via}} | -candidate_file <file_name> } [ -exclude_local_grid {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} | -candidate_file <file_name> ] [ -exclude {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} | -candidate_file <file_name> ] [ -threshold <float> | -candidate_file <file_name> ] [ -area {x1, y1, x2, y2} | -candidate_file <file_name> ]

options.name: -exclude
options.description: {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} Exclude areas in which the part of stripes is not trimming

options.name: -exclude_local_grid
options.description: {{lx1 ly1 ux1 uy1} {lx2 ly2 ux2 uy2}...} Exclude areas in which the whole stripes are skipped for trimming

options.name: -keep_dangling_via
options.description: Keep dangling VIAs when trimming stripe

options.name: -layer
options.description: One layer name for stripe trimming and two layer names for VIA trimming

options.name: -net
options.description: Specify the net names to be trimmed ((net)+, required)

options.name: -type
options.description: Trim type, to trim stripe on one layer or stack via vetween two layers
